title: Jeonnam Bids for $1.9Bn National Hydrogen Complex with Plan to Build Koreaâ€™s Biggest Clean Hâ‚‚ Hub
content: South Koreaâ€™s Jeonnam Province (South Jeolla) has formally applied to the national government for KRW 2.7 trillion ($1.9 billion) in state subsidies to build what it says would be the largest clean hydrogen production complex in the country, centered on a "Clean Hydrogen Energy Industry Belt" stretching along the southwest coast. The proposed facility would begin with a 500MW electrolyser installation and expand to 1GW, powered by both carbon-free nuclear energy from the 5.2GW Hanbit Nuclear Power Plant â€” the only one on Koreaâ€™s southwest coast â€” and the regionâ€™s abundant renewable energy resources.
author: Analysts Account
